description_long: This object is a long, slender knife or dagger with a straight, double-edged blade that tapers to a sharp point. The blade appears metallic and is complemented by a short, cylindrical crossguard that separates it from the handle. The handle is rounded, ribbed for grip, and ends in a rounded pommel, likely made of the same or similar metal as the blade. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible ornamentation or decorative features. The finish is dark and matte, suggesting a functional, possibly tactical or military style. The proportions and construction indicate it is designed for effective thrusting and cutting.
description: A straight, double-edged knife with a ribbed metal handle and a short crossguard, finished in a dark matte tone. The design is utilitarian and unadorned.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the straight, double-edged blade and the ribbed handle with a rounded pommel.
description_view_1: Edge-on view highlighting the thinness of the blade and the rounded shape of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, showing the blade, crossguard, and handle details.
description_view_3: Top-down view displaying the width of the handle and the narrow profile of the blade.
attribute_handle_design: rounded, ribbed metal handle with a rounded pommel
